summary: DHH argues that ADHD is massively overdiagnosed, comparing the diagnostic process to cold reading by psychics. He points out that the commonly listed ADHD symptoms—forgetfulness, attraction to novelty, difficulty with habits—are universal human experiences, not pathologies. He notes the explosion of Adderall prescriptions (45 million in 2023, up from 35 million in 2019) as evidence of a cottage industry convincing normal people they have a medical condition. He concludes that people want a medical explanation for ordinary human struggles rather than accepting mundane solutions like better sleep and exercise.
key_points: Common ADHD symptom lists describe universal human experiences like forgetfulness and attraction to novelty, not distinct pathology
The ADHD diagnostic criteria are so broad that most people could qualify, undermining their usefulness as a clinical standard
Adderall prescriptions jumped from 35 million to 45 million between 2019 and 2023, suggesting overdiagnosis
The ADHD diagnosis industry functions like cold reading—throwing out vague enough symptoms that everyone finds hits
People prefer a medical label over mundane lifestyle fixes like sleep, exercise, and diet
money_quotes: No wonder we've seen an explosion of ADHD diagnosis. This list applies to most humans at least part of the time!
You don't need a diagnosis to be a flawed human. It goes for all of us.
There clearly is also an entire cottage industry cropping up around convincing perfectly normal people that they suffer from ADHD.
I think a lot of people desperately want to hear that there's a medical reason for why they sometimes can't focus, don't feel motivated, forget things, or find breaking bad habits hard.
If you want to supercharge your morning's productivity routine by popping a pill or two of amphetamines, own it! Don't hide behind some label.
Sure, you may recognize all those symptoms, but for true ADHD sufferers, they're just, like, worse! Okay, but what kind of diagnostic standard is that?!
key_insight: The broad, universally relatable nature of ADHD symptom lists functions like psychic cold reading, enabling a cottage industry that medicalizes ordinary human imperfection.
